question.text: In the AIF ecosystem, investors in overseas markets are commonly known as:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In overseas markets, AIF investors are known as Limited Partners (LPs) as AIFs are mostly floated as partnerships. LPs provide capital but have limited liability and limited management role. General Partners (GPs) are the investment managers. **The Trap:** GPs are the fund managers, not investors. The LP/GP distinction is fundamental to understanding AIF partnership structures. In India, investors are called 'unit holders' or 'contributors' in a trust structure. **Cross-Exam Context:** LP/GP terminology appears across all XIX-series exams. Series XXI-B (Portfolio Managers) also references this distinction when comparing PMS with AIF structures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When working with international investors or presenting to family offices familiar with global PE, use LP/GP terminology. For domestic HNI clients new to AIFs, explain the trust structure terminology (unit holders, sponsors, managers) alongside the international equivalents.
